subject: french
explainer: "Je m'appelle" literally means "I call myself". Use it to introduce yourself.

subject: french
prompt: How do you say "I am 12 years old" in French?
explainer: In French you HAVE your age — "j'ai" ("I have") not "je suis" ("I am").

subject: french
explainer: Douze = 12. Useful sequence: dix, onze, douze, treize, quatorze, quinze.

subject: french
explainer: Frère = brother. Sister is sœur. Petit frère = little brother.

subject: french
explainer: Ma sœur = my sister. "Ma" is feminine "my"; brother would use "mon".

subject: french
explainer: Mai = May. Easy to remember — almost the same word.

subject: french
prompt: What is the French word for "September"?
explainer: Septembre. Most months are similar to English: janvier, février, mars, avril, mai...

subject: french
prompt: How do you write "30" in French?
explainer: Trente = 30. Don't confuse with treize (13).

subject: french
explainer: Vingt = 20, deux = 2, so vingt-deux = 22.

subject: french
prompt: What does "je suis de Paris" mean?
explainer: "Je suis de" = "I am from". Use it for the place you come from.

subject: french
explainer: Soixante (60) + dix (10) = 70. French builds 70 as "sixty-ten".

subject: french
prompt: What does "j'ai les cheveux blonds" mean?
explainer: Cheveux = hair (always plural). Blonds = blonde.

subject: french
prompt: How do you say "I have green eyes" in French?
explainer: Yeux = eyes. Verts = green (plural to match les yeux).

subject: french
prompt: What does "courts" mean when describing hair?
explainer: Courts = short (cheveux courts). Long is "longs".

subject: french
explainer: Une barbe = a beard. Une moustache = a moustache.

subject: french
prompt: What does "je porte des lunettes" mean?
explainer: Porter = to wear. Lunettes = glasses (always plural in French).

subject: french
prompt: "J'ai les yeux marron." What colour are the eyes?
explainer: Marron = brown. Strange feature: "marron" doesn't change for plural — always "yeux marron".

subject: french
prompt: What does "je vis dans une grande maison" mean?
options: I live in a big house
explainer: Vivre = to live. "Je vis" = "I live". Grande = big (feminine, to match maison).

subject: french
explainer: Un appartement = a flat. Une maison = a house.

subject: french
explainer: La côte = the coast. Dans le centre = in the centre. Dans la banlieue = on the outskirts.

subject: french
explainer: Grand-mère = grandmother. Grand-père = grandfather.

subject: french
prompt: How do you say "I get on well with my brother"?
options: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère
Je vais bien avec mon frère
Je suis bien avec mon frère
explainer: Je m'entends bien avec... = I get on well with... "Je m'entends mal avec..." = I don't get on well with...

subject: french
explainer: Mon oncle = my uncle. Ma tante = my aunty.

subject: french
prompt: Choose the correct form: "Ma sœur est ___" (tall).
explainer: Sœur is feminine singular, so the adjective takes -e: "grande". For a brother (masculine) it would be "grand".

subject: french
prompt: Choose the correct form: "Mon père est ___" (fun).
explainer: Père is masculine singular, so the adjective stays in its base form: "marrant".

subject: french
prompt: How do you say "shy" (masculine) in French?
explainer: Timide = shy. Useful: "timide" doesn't change for masculine vs feminine — same form.

subject: french
prompt: What do "très / assez / un peu" mean?
explainer: These are intensifiers. "Mon frère est très grand" = "My brother is very tall".

subject: french
prompt: In French, which verb do you use to talk about age?
explainer: In French you HAVE your age. "J'ai treize ans" literally means "I have thirteen years".

subject: french
explainer: Elle = she. Il = he. Je = I. Tu = you (informal).

subject: french
options: Je ne porte pas des lunettes.
Je ne porte pas de lunettes.
Je ne porte des lunettes pas.
explainer: In the negative, "des" or "une" change to "de" before the noun. So "Je ne porte pas DE lunettes".

subject: french
explainer: Ma mère = my mother. Mon père = my father.

subject: french
prompt: "Dans ma famille, il y a quatre personnes." What does this say?
options: In my family there are four people
My family is in four places
explainer: "Il y a" = there is / there are. So: "In my family, there are four people."

subject: french
explainer: Mon cousin = my (male) cousin. Female cousin = ma cousine.

subject: french
explainer: Un chien = a dog. Un chat = a cat. Useful for the pets topic.

subject: french
explainer: Habiter = to live (in a place). "J'habite à..." + city. "Je suis de..." = I'm from...

subject: french
explainer: La campagne = the countryside. Other useful: la ville (the city), la montagne (the mountain), la côte (the coast).

subject: french
prompt: Which is a French-speaking country in West Africa?
explainer: Sénégal is one of many French-speaking African countries (also Côte d'Ivoire, Mali, Cameroun).

subject: french
explainer: Petite = small (feminine, agreeing with maison). Big = grande. New = nouvelle. Old = vieille.

subject: french
explainer: Sympa (short for sympathique) = nice or friendly. Same form for masculine and feminine.

subject: french
explainer: Méchant(e) = mean, nasty, naughty. Opposite of sympa or gentil.

subject: french
prompt: Which sentence means "I like my brother because he is funny"?
options: Je n'aime pas mon frère parce qu'il est marrant
J'aime mon frère parce qu'il est marrant
Mon frère aime parce qu'il est marrant
explainer: J'aime = I like. Parce que = because. Marrant = funny. Useful pattern: "opinion + parce que + reason".

subject: french
explainer: Drôle = funny. Same form for masculine and feminine. Marrant(e) is another common word for funny.
